panels.description: Close-up: Victor Frankenstein’s face is contorted in a mix of horror and sudden realization. Rain streams down his face, eyes wide with pupils dilated in terror. He is clutching a gnarled tree trunk in a dark forest, his knuckles white. Dramatic lightning-cast shadows highlight his chattering teeth.

bubbles.text: I shuddered at the conception of the murderer being my brother.

panels.description: Wide shot: A massive, grotesque silhouette of the creature is seen scaling a steep, jagged mountain peak with inhuman agility. The figure is a blur of motion against the gloomy, stormy sky. In the foreground, Victor is a small, shadowed figure looking up in despair.

bubbles.text: I thought of pursuing the devil... but he soon reached the summit and disappeared.

panels.description: Medium shot: Victor stands motionless in a torrential downpour. The sky is pitch black, illuminated only by a distant, fading flash of lightning. He looks physically broken, his head bowed as the rain soaks through his heavy coat. The atmosphere is one of total isolation and anguish.

bubbles.text: The scene was enveloped in an impenetrable darkness.

panels.description: Medium close-up: Victor stares into the distance with a look of profound guilt and horror. The realization of his creation's nature weighs heavily on him. The lighting is low-key, with heavy cross-hatching to emphasize the intensity of his despair. He looks like a man haunted by a demon of his own making.

bubbles.text: I considered the being whom I had cast among mankind... endowed with the will and power to effect purposes of horror.

panels.description: Wide shot: The first light of a cold dawn breaks over the horizon, revealing the distant silhouette of Geneva. Victor is walking along a muddy road, looking exhausted and disheveled. The pale morning light casts long, lonely shadows across the path.

bubbles.text: I well knew that if I told this to anyone, I should have been looked upon as insane.

panels.description: Medium shot: Victor stands before the heavy wooden front door of his father's house. He looks hesitant, his hand hovering over the iron knocker. His expression is one of doubt and secrecy. The architecture is grand but feels oppressive in the dim morning light.

Close-up: A framed portrait of Caroline Beaufort hanging on a dark wood wall. She wears rustic clothing, her face pale but beautiful, projecting an air of dignity. Victor’s reflection is faintly visible in the glass of the frame, looking weary. The final shot transitions to a view of the road leading away to Ingolstadt.

bubbles.text: I gazed on the picture of my mother... then I returned to Ingolstadt.
